text: This is a heads up that this video contains footage from surgery so you will be able to see certain organs and blood so do not watch if you're under 18.
This is a video demonstrating how a staple gun is used to remove a wedge of lung during cancer surgery.
What you can see on the screen is a staple gun which is attached to a da Vinci robot.
This is a robotic surgery and it works by clamping the affected piece of lung.
The staple will then cut down the midline of the lung and fire staples on either side of the cut to ensure there's no bleeding from any vessels.
Surgeons do this throughout the entire piece of tissue and then it's removed and that is how a lung cancer is resected.
